v1.headline: Doctor Shift & On-Call Scheduling,
v1.subheadline: The simplest way to plan hospital on-call duty and shifts. Configure your teams, set the rules, and let PlanGarzi build a fair, compliant monthly schedule — ready to export.

seo.sectionTitle: The complete solution for medical on-call scheduling
seo.garziDesc: PlanGarzi automates on-call planning for any hospital department. You define teams, mark leave days, and the algorithm builds the monthly schedule while respecting mandatory rest periods (24h after day shifts, 48h after night shifts) and the 48h weekly cap. Building the on-call rota takes seconds, not hours in Excel.
seo.tureDesc: Whether you run 12-hour day/night shifts or 24-hour on-call duty, PlanGarzi distributes shifts fairly across doctors. It accounts for the monthly minimum norm (7h × working days), individual preferences and rotating teams. The result: a transparent, conflict-free shift plan that's easy to adjust manually when needed.
seo.legeDesc: The algorithm follows the rules common in hospitals: team rotation, floating doctors filling gaps, automatic norm equalization and clear reporting of conflicts or understaffed days. Export the schedule as PDF for posting or as Excel for reporting, and email each doctor a personal view link.

demo.gridCaption: The monthly grid view — see every doctor's shifts at a glance
demo.configCaption: Team configuration — organize doctors into teams with custom rules
demo.captionLine: 5 doctors · 4 rotating teams · D-N-R-R cadence · 1 floating doctor
demo.metaTitle: Live demo: medical shift scheduling | PlanGarzi
demo.metaDescription: See a real example of an automatically generated shift roster — rotating teams, fair distribution, no signup required.
demo.h1: Demo: an automatically generated shift schedule
demo.subtitle: A real example of how PlanGarzi schedules a doctor team across an entire month.
demo.intro: Below is a sample shift schedule for 4 rotating teams (one doctor each) plus a floating doctor who covers leave gaps, generated automatically for November 2025. The schedule honours mandatory rest — 24 hours after a day shift, 48 hours after a night shift — and the 48-hour weekly cap. The same algorithm powers PlanGarzi for any hospital: define your teams, mark leave days, and the generator builds the month in seconds. This live demo is fully visible to any visitor — no account needed — so you can preview the result before trying it on your own data.
demo.explainerColumns: Each row is one doctor; each column is one day of the month. The colored dot next to the name marks the team (the 4 teams rotate through a D-N-R-R pattern: day, night, two days off). The right-hand bar shows the delta vs. the monthly norm (140 hours for November).
demo.explainerColors: Blue = day shift (08:00–20:00). Indigo = night shift (20:00–08:00). Orange = leave (Dr. Elena, Nov 12–14). The yellow ring marks shifts manually overridden on top of the auto-generated ones. The floating doctor (F badge) steps in to cover gaps left by leave.
demo.explainerHolidays: Columns with a green background are public holidays (here, Nov 30 — St. Andrew's Day). Grey columns are weekends. The algorithm factors both into the monthly hours and ensures coverage with no rest conflicts.
demo.ctaTitle: Want your own schedule, generated automatically?

faqs.q: How long does it take to generate a monthly schedule?
faqs.a: The algorithm builds the monthly schedule in seconds regardless of team size. For a department with 20 doctors and complex constraints, the maximum runtime stays under 10 seconds.
Yes. Cancel from the billing page via the Stripe Customer Portal or directly from your PlanGarzi account. Access remains active until the end of the paid period, no penalties.

faqs.q: Does it work for any hospital department?
faqs.a: Yes. PlanGarzi supports 12-hour shifts (day/night), 24-hour on-call duty and combinations. You configure doctors per shift, rest rules and rotating teams per department.
Yes — 90 full days with full feature access. No credit card required to start. You receive a notification 7 days before the trial expires.
Data is stored encrypted in Supabase (PostgreSQL) with authentication-gated access. GDPR compliant. You can delete your account and all data anytime from the account page.

faqs.q: How does PDF / Excel export work?
faqs.a: One click from the grid view: pick the month and format (PDF for posting, Excel for reporting). You can also email the schedule directly to doctors — each gets a personal view link.

faqs.q: How many doctors and teams can I add?
faqs.a: No limit. The subscription includes unlimited doctors, teams and schedule months. Price is fixed regardless of hospital size.

faqs.q: Can I edit the schedule manually after generation?
faqs.a: Yes. Any cell can be edited manually. Manual edits are marked as 'pinned' and preserved on subsequent regenerations. Rest conflicts appear as visual warnings.

featuresPage.metaTitle: PlanGarzi Features — hospital on-call schedule generator
featuresPage.metaDescription: All PlanGarzi features: automatic monthly schedule generation, rest conflict detection, shift equalization, leave management, PDF/Excel export and email delivery.
featuresPage.subtitle: Everything you need to plan a department's on-call schedule in half an hour instead of two days.

items.title: Automatic on-call schedule generation
items.body: The cadence-first algorithm builds the monthly schedule in seconds. You configure the teams, rest rules and per-doctor availability — the rest is automatic. The schedule respects a day-night-rest-rest cadence per team, distributes shifts fairly between doctors and fills gaps with smart rotation. For a department with 20 doctors and 30 days, generation runs in just a few seconds. You can regenerate anytime: manual edits stay pinned and are preserved on subsequent regenerations.

items.title: Rest conflict detection (24h day / 48h night / 48h week)
items.body: Every rest conflict appears immediately as a visual warning on the affected cell. The algorithm enforces the legal rules automatically: 24h rest after day shifts, 48h after night shifts, max 48h of work per week per doctor. If you force a shift that breaks a rule, you get a clear warning: conflict type, doctor and date. This way you avoid scheduling errors that lead to fatigue, accidents or compliance issues during inspections.

items.title: Shift equalization between doctors
items.body: After generation, the equalization engine swaps shifts between doctors with equivalent norms to balance the +/- deltas against the monthly target. No doctor ends up with more nights than another without reason. The algorithm accounts for floating doctors, constrained teams and individual preferences. Result: a fair schedule, no favoritism, no overtime piling up on a few people. Per-doctor stats — hours worked, day/night shifts, delta vs norm — are shown next to the calendar grid.

items.title: Leave and bridge day management
items.body: Mark each doctor's leave days from a simple calendar. The algorithm excludes those doctors from scheduling and adjusts the monthly norm. Bridge days — weekends and public holidays caught between leave periods — are detected and can be treated either as extra rest or as workdays, by preference. Each doctor sees how many leave days they have left, with validation to prevent exceeding the legal limit. National holidays are imported automatically for the current year.

items.title: PDF and Excel export
items.body: Export the monthly schedule with one click as PDF (for posting on the bulletin board) or Excel (for HR reporting). The PDF includes department name, month, doctor list and color-coded grids per team. Excel exports the data in tabular form, easy to filter and sort. Both formats follow pagination rules for A4 or A3 print. You can also export per-doctor stats: hours worked, day/night shifts and norm deltas for monthly reporting.

items.title: Email delivery with personal view link
items.body: With a single click you send the monthly schedule to all doctors — each gets an email with a personal view link. The link doesn't require login: the doctor opens it and sees their own shifts for the month, in mobile-friendly format. Links are unique per doctor, cryptographically signed and expire at the end of the month. This way doctors stop calling the coordinator asking 'when am I working?'. You update the schedule, they instantly receive the new details.

items.title: Rotating team configuration
items.body: Define how many teams you want, the color of each and their rotation order. The algorithm applies a staggered day-night-rest-rest cadence: team A starts Monday morning, team B Monday night, team C Tuesday morning and so on. This way you cover days with the fixed number of doctors per shift without unwanted overlap. You can define constrained teams (only senior members, for instance) or flexible teams with free rotation. Each team has its own set of doctors, color and rules.

items.title: Floating doctors
items.body: Floating doctors don't belong to a fixed team — the algorithm uses them to fill the gaps left after the main rotation finishes. Ideal for residents, part-time doctors or specialists who cover several departments. You configure each one's availability (preferred days, days to avoid) and the algorithm distributes them automatically where needed, prioritizing norm balance. This way you cover gaps without disrupting main team rotation and without overloading a few doctors.

items.title: Click & drag editing
items.body: Edit the grid like a spreadsheet: hold the mouse button on a cell, drag across multiple days or doctors and apply the same shift to the entire selected area. For quick changes at the week or team level, click & drag is far faster than editing cell by cell. The same gesture works for deletion: select the area and clear it in one move. Ideal when reorganizing the schedule after last-minute changes, when a whole team swaps rotation or when marking long leave periods.

items.title: Undo / redo history
items.body: Every manual change is saved to history. Press Ctrl+Z to undo the last change, Ctrl+Y to redo it. Works for individual edits, for selections made via click & drag and for full schedule regenerations. You can experiment freely: try a variant, if you don't like it revert to the previous one without losing the hours of work invested. History persists for the session and covers tens of steps back, enough for any monthly planning task.

banner.title: We use cookies
banner.description: We use essential cookies to run the app and, optionally, analytics cookies to understand how the site is used. You can accept, reject or manage your preferences.

dialog.title: Cookie preferences
dialog.description: Choose which categories of cookies you allow. Strictly necessary cookies cannot be disabled — they are essential for the app to work.

necessary.name: Strictly necessary
necessary.description: Cookies for authentication, session, language preference and remembering this choice. The app does not work without them.

analytics.name: Analytics
analytics.description: Vercel Analytics — measures anonymous page traffic to help us improve the product. Off by default.

privacy.metaDescription: How PlanGarzi collects, uses and protects your personal data. Your rights under GDPR and our cookie policy.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains what personal data we collect, why, on what legal basis, who we share it with, and what rights you have under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

controller.title: Data controller
controller.body: PlanGarzi is the controller of the data collected through this site. For any request relating to your data, contact us via the contact form or at contact@plangarzi.ro.

data.title: What we collect
data.intro: We collect only the data needed to provide the service and meet legal obligations:

doctors.label: Doctors
doctors.body: name, email (optional), shift preferences — added by you to generate the schedule.

schedules.label: Schedules
schedules.body: generated shifts, leave days, bridge days, public holidays, teams.

billing.label: Billing
billing.body: Stripe customer ID, subscription ID, status and period. Payments are processed by Stripe — we do not store card details.

contact.label: Contact
contact.body: name, email, message — when you submit the contact form.

purposes.title: Purposes of processing
purposes.body: We use this data to provide the on-call scheduling service, for billing through Stripe, to respond to support requests and to keep the application secure.

legalBasis.title: Legal basis
legalBasis.contract: Performance of contract — for account creation, schedule generation and billing.
legalBasis.legitimate: Legitimate interest — for security, abuse prevention and replying to contact requests.
legalBasis.consent: Consent — for optional analytics cookies (Vercel Analytics). You can withdraw consent at any time.

processors.title: Processors (sub-processors)
processors.stripe: Stripe — payment processing and subscription management.
processors.resend: Resend — transactional email delivery (account confirmation, password resets, schedule sharing).
processors.vercel: Vercel — hosting and (optionally, with consent) Vercel Analytics.

retention.title: Retention period
retention.body: We keep your data for as long as your account is active. When you delete your account from /account we permanently remove all associated data (doctors, teams, shifts, leave days, holidays, shared links). Stripe may retain billing data to meet legal obligations (e.g. 10 years for accounting).

rights.title: Your rights
rights.intro: Under GDPR you have the following rights:
rights.access: Access — find out what data we hold about you.
rights.erase: Erasure — ask us to delete your data.
rights.portability: Portability — receive your data in a structured format.
rights.object: Object — object to processing based on legitimate interest.
rights.withdraw: Withdraw consent — for optional cookies, at any time, via the button below.
rights.complaint: Complaint — to the Romanian DPA (ANSPDCP, www.dataprotection.ro) or your local supervisory authority.

exercise.title: How to exercise your rights
exercise.body: You can delete your account and all data directly from the account page. For any other request, use the contact form:

cookies.title: Cookies we use
cookies.intro: We use a minimal set of cookies and local storage. The table below lists everything in use:
